articles.headline: Businesses Scaling AI Initatives: From Layoffs to ROI and New Hiring Waves
articles.summary: Companies are shifting from using AI as a justification for layoffs to actively expanding access to AI tools, with Santander reporting over €35 million in AI investment returns in Q1 2026. The narrative is evolving toward execution, with major firms like GM integrating collaborative robots while simultaneously expanding AI access to 185,000 employees globally.

articles.headline: UK Cybersecurity Firm Rubrik Announces $500 Million Investment and London as European HQ
articles.summary: U.S. cybersecurity and data resilience company Rubrik is investing more than $500 million over five years in the UK, establishing London as its European headquarters. This move signals strong growth in the European cybersecurity market and provides a strategic infrastructure boost for businesses facing increasing data resilience and cyber threat challenges.

articles.headline: Apple Raises MacBook and iPad Prices Due to AI-Driven Memory and Storage Chip Costs
articles.summary: Apple has increased prices on popular products like the MacBook and iPad due to rising costs of memory and storage chips sparked by the AI boom. This directly impacts operational budgets for businesses reliant on Apple hardware, signaling broader inflationary pressures in the tech supply chain driven by AI infrastructure demand.
